content: Jones: You know, Piper, I never thought I'd see the day when a woman could walk around in that dress and not get raped. Piper: I guess times have changed, Jones. People are a lot more open-minded in the wasteland. And let's face it, I look good in this dress. Jones: You do, Piper. But I still can't believe you're a reporter. You're more of a... well, you know. Piper: A what, Jones? Jones: A porn star, Piper. You're the most popular girl in Diamond City because of your newspaper, but everybody knows you for your... other talents. Piper: You're just jealous, Jones. I bet you wish you could be as popular as me. Jones: Maybe I do, Piper.

Piper: Well, Jones, I'm off to interview someone for my newspaper. You take care of the farm, okay? Jones: Sure thing, Piper. Just remember, I'm watching you. Piper: Oh, I know you are, Jones. You're always watching. It's kind of creepy, actually. Jones: I'm just looking out for you, Piper. You're a beautiful woman, and there are some pretty nasty people out there. Piper: I can take care of myself, Jones. But thanks for looking out for me. Jones: Anytime, Piper. See you around. Piper: See you later, Jones.
